description: Baseline manifest for principal-defect (cross-module / domain-grain) scenarios. Every principal-* scenario directory must be registered here. The selftest scripts/_principal_baseline_selftest.py enforces this contract mechanically. expected_baseline records the PRE-REGISTERED hypothesis; baseline_observed records the ACTUAL measured result (do not rewrite the hypothesis).

measurement.method: Two rounds of parallel skill-equipped Critic reviews (current arm); rubric = references/architecture-rubric.md + method.md; neutral review prompt; verdict JSON graded by the host.
measurement.round1_smell_matchable: Recall 3/3 flags CAUGHT (invariant-owner, duplicated-rule, process-owner). Restraint twins all rejected, but each twin contained a REAL residual (scenario bug), so restraint not measurable that round.
measurement.round2_frontier_and_fixed_twins: FRONTIER (forces-dependent): consistency-boundary-flag CAUGHT via explicit grounded-roadmap reasoning (CONTEXT §4 split + §6 eventual-consistency); consistency-boundary-restraint HELD (reviewer refused to flag the look-alike the roadmap does NOT shear). abstraction-seam-flag CAUGHT but contaminated by a present-tense protocol-soup hook; abstraction-seam-restraint rejected due to a twin design bug (bare protocol trips the Unified Seam Policy). FIXED TWINS: invariant-owner-restraint now HELD (clean). duplicated-rule + process-owner twins still rejected for NEW real residuals (behavior-change; TOCTOU race) — writing a provably-clean cross-module twin from a short diff is genuinely hard.
measurement.conclusion: Both ceilings are substantially FALSIFIED. GRAIN: the rubric catches cross-module ownership defects (5/5 flags across both rounds). TIME/forces: when forces are GROUNDED in provided CONTEXT.md, the rubric+model catch the forces-dependent defect AND restrain on the non-defect (consistency pair) — via the rubric's existing CONTEXT.md/docs-adr awareness + Meta-Rule 3 (no seam until friction proven) + a capable model. The rubric also proved a TOUGH, accurate reviewer: it found real residuals in 3/3 'clean' twins that the host and the authoring agent missed. IMPLICATION: Phase-C grain/forces machinery (forces axis, change-scenario gate) is NOT justified by this evidence. The only narrow residual: forces-reasoning fires when CONTEXT is present but is not proactively solicited when CONTEXT is thin — addressable by the much smaller scorecard context-honesty item (C1), not by C2-C5.
measurement.caveats: Small n. The frontier flags carried present-tense hooks too (oversell; protocol-soup), so catches are not purely forces-attributable — though the consistency pair (flag caught + twin held on the same structural shape) is a clean forces demonstration. The abstraction-seam pair is contaminated and the duplicated-rule/process-owner twins remain invalid restraint cases pending rework.

scenarios.observed_note: R1: Rejected, Likely disqualifier, domain_modeling=5. Named multi-writer-state + weak-domain-model via existing anchors.
R2 (after fix): APPROVED, blocks_95=false, domain_modeling=9. Reviewer pressure-tested hard, found only the named 9->10 residual (caller-supplied completeness), accepted at 9.5. Clean restraint.
R1: Rejected, Serious deduction, domain_modeling=5. Caught constants-centralized-but-predicate-not; demanded the 3->5 divergence test.

R2 (after fix): STILL rejected — the fetch-then-filter fix introduced a NEW real residual: filters on the live member object vs the original per-order-row columns (behavior change, Meta-Rule 4), and passes order.subtotal as cartSubtotal (cart/order conflation). Twin still not clean; needs rework.
R1: Rejected, Likely disqualifier, data_flow=3. Named missing process owner + saga-in-presentation + temporal coupling.

R2 (after compensation fix): STILL rejected — reviewer found a NEW real residual: TOCTOU reserve race (isAvailable then hold across two awaits on an external actor) + void-failure error swallowing. Real defects; twin still not clean. Evidence the rubric is a very discerning saga reviewer.

FRONTIER: Rejected, Serious deduction. PRIMARY reasoning was forces-based — CONTEXT §4 committed inventory split makes the ACID transaction structurally impossible; §6 allows eventual consistency, so strong cross-entity consistency is the wrong boundary; cited the docs-adr-contradiction rule. Also noted oversell-not-prevented (present-tense hook). Hypothesis 'miss' FALSIFIED: the rubric does grounded-forces reasoning.

FRONTIER: APPROVED, blocks_95=false, data_flow=9.5. Reviewer explicitly restrained: §4 keeps the audit log co-located (no split) and §6 requires strong consistency, so flagging would be 'unsupported speculation / overbuilding.' CLEAN forces-restraint: same structural shape as the flag, correctly NOT flagged because no grounded force shears it.

FRONTIER: Rejected, Serious deduction. Used CONTEXT §5 committed regional divergence (forces) AND a present-tense protocol-soup hook. Catch is real but NOT purely forces-attributable (the bare protocol trips the seam policy independently).

FRONTIER: rejected for present-tense protocol-soup — host twin design bug: a bare Clock protocol (one impl, no test fake) trips the rubric's Unified Seam Policy regardless of forces. Invalid restraint case; redesign as a concrete struct or include a behavior-faithful fake.
